behaviors.description: Renamed files are reported only in `renamed`, not double-counted as `added` + `deleted`.
behaviors.justification: Forcing phrase: "reads a git repository" and behavior text "Renamed files". The real failure boundary is git rename semantics plus parser/category logic across a real subprocess result. Adjacent-lower miss-proof: an Integration test with mocked diff/status text can pass while missing real rename encodings (e.g., similarity-prefixed rename status, path quoting), so mock data can hide double-count bugs. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML call in behavior text, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp repo, commit a file, rename it with `git mv`, run the CLI against the repo, and assert report has rename count/path and zero corresponding add/delete entries for that same file.

behaviors.description: Binary-file diffs do not crash processing; binary files are included in counts.
behaviors.justification: Forcing phrase: "Diffs that include binary files". This depends on real git diff output/exit behavior for binary content and the tool’s subprocess decoding path. Adjacent-lower miss-proof: stubbing subprocess output with plain text can miss real binary-diff markers/edge formatting and decode paths that trigger crashes. Rejected higher levels: no model judgment or multi-agent pipeline involved.
behaviors.test_description: Create a repo with a committed binary file, modify it, run the CLI, assert process exits successfully and report includes the binary file in the appropriate category/count.

behaviors.description: On a repo with no commits, tool returns an empty report instead of erroring.
behaviors.justification: Forcing phrase: "repository that has no commits yet". The failure is tied to real git initial-state behavior (e.g., commands against unborn HEAD) via subprocess. Adjacent-lower miss-proof: mocking command responses can hide exact error codes/messages and branch-state behavior that the CLI must handle. Rejected higher levels: behavior is deterministic infrastructure handling, not AI-driven.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with no commits, run the CLI, assert zero exit failure and an empty structured report on stdout.

behaviors.description: Reported counts match the repository’s true change history.
behaviors.justification: Forcing phrase: "match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed". This is end-to-end correctness against real git history traversal and categorization, crossing subprocess + filesystem + parser boundaries. Adjacent-lower miss-proof: fixture-based integration tests can validate logic on curated cases but miss discrepancies from real git history semantics/order/rename detection thresholds. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML dependency or agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a temp repo with a known sequence of commits containing add/delete/rename/modify events, run the CLI for that history window, and assert category counts equal the known ground truth from the scripted history.

behaviors.description: Output is identical when analyzing staged vs unstaged changes.
behaviors.justification: Forcing phrase: "staged or unstaged". Correctness depends on real git index vs working-tree behavior and CLI subprocess interactions. Adjacent-lower miss-proof: mocked git responses can force equality and miss real differences in index/worktree command paths, causing false confidence. Rejected higher levels: behavior does not involve AI outputs or multiple agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, create equivalent change sets once as unstaged and once staged, run the CLI in both states, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured stdout.
